code:
class Solution:
    def longestPalindrome(self, s: str) -> str:
        if not s:
            return ""

        # Preprocess the string to handle even and odd length palindromes uniformly
        # For example, "aba" becomes "^#a#b#a#$"
        # "abba" becomes "^#a#b#b#a#$"
        T = "^#" + "#".join(s) + "#$"
        n = len(T)
        P = [0] * n  # P[i] stores the radius of the palindrome centered at i
        
        C = 0  # Center of the current palindrome that extends farthest to the right
        R = 0  # Right boundary of the current palindrome (C + P[C])

        max_len = 0  # Max radius of the palindrome found so far
        max_center = 0 # Center of the longest palindrome found so far

        for i in range(1, n - 1):
            # Calculate the mirror index of i relative to C
            i_mirror = 2 * C - i

            # If i is within the right boundary R, we can initialize P[i]
            # using the mirror property
            if i < R:
                P[i] = min(R - i, P[i_mirror])
            else:
                P[i] = 0 # i is outside R, no mirror info, start with radius 0

            # Attempt to expand the palindrome centered at i
            # Check characters symmetrically around i
            while T[i + 1 + P[i]] == T[i - 1 - P[i]]:
                P[i] += 1

            # If the palindrome centered at i extends beyond R,
            # update C and R
            if i + P[i] > R:
                C = i
                R = i + P[i]

            # Update max_len and max_center if a new longest palindrome is found
            if P[i] > max_len:
                max_len = P[i]
                max_center = i
        
        # Calculate the starting index of the longest palindrome in the original string s
        # (max_center - max_len) gives the starting index in T (e.g., for "^#a#b#a#$", if max_center=4, max_len=3,
        # then (4-3)=1 which is the index of the first '#'.
        # Subtract 1 to account for the leading '^' and divide by 2 to convert to s index.
        start_index_s = (max_center - max_len - 1) // 2
        
        # The length of the longest palindrome in s is simply max_len
        return s[start_index_s : start_index_s + max_len]
prompt: Manacher's algorithm for longest palindromic substring yaz.
